title: Commented-out dead code in fu-asus-hid-device.c
description: Code is commented out and flagged with a TODO. It should be removed or uncommented if fixed.
rationale: Code is explicitly commented out with a TODO, indicating it is not currently used but might be needed or should be cleaned up.

title: Potential file descriptor leak in fu-polkit-agent.c
description: Explicit TODO to close file descriptors before exec to avoid leaking them to the agent process.
rationale: Closing file descriptors before fork/exec is a standard security and resource management practice to prevent leaks.

title: Incorrect enum representation in fu-bnr-dp.rs
description: FIXME suggests using u4 representation for packed struct correctness.
rationale: The comment explicitly states the representation should be u4 and the struct layout needs adjustment, indicating a known technical debt or bug.

title: Incorrect enum layout in fu-bnr-dp.rs
description: FIXME suggests removing the enum and using bitfields for error/busy bits to match hardware spec.
rationale: The comment highlights a discrepancy between the current implementation and the desired bitfield layout.

title: Conditionalize boot order modification in fu-uefi-bootmgr.c
description: TODO suggests that modifying boot order might need to be conditional on UEFI version.
rationale: Unconditional modification of boot order might cause issues on some systems, as indicated by the TODO.

title: High complexity in fu_engine_install_loop
description: Function is long (~180 lines) and handles multiple phases of installation (detach, write, attach, reload).
rationale: Splitting this complex logic into smaller helper functions would improve readability and maintainability.

title: Check for unfinished operations in Nordic HID
description: TODO suggests a missing check for pending operations before starting a new one.
rationale: Missing check could lead to race conditions or errors if the device is busy.

title: Missing overridable methods in Logitech HID++ class
description: TODO indicates the class was intended to have virtual methods for extensibility.
rationale: Defining virtual methods allows subclasses to customize behavior, which is currently missing as per the TODO.
